steps: Go to your Canvas instance (e.g. https://canvas.instructure.com or your institution's Canvas URL)
Click your account name or profile icon in the top-right corner
Scroll down to the 'Approved Integrations' section
Enter a purpose/description for the token (e.g. 'API Integration')
Optionally set an expiration date (leave blank for no expiration)
Copy the token immediately — it will not be shown again after you close the dialog

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Token
credential_fields.description: Your Canvas LMS personal access token

notes: Free Canvas accounts available at canvas.instructure.com
Institutional Canvas deployments may have custom URLs (e.g. yourschool.instructure.com)
The access token is shown only once — copy it before closing the dialog
Token acts as your user — it has the same permissions as your Canvas account role
The base URL for API calls is {canvas_base_url}/api/v1/ (varies by institution)
Rate limit: typically 3,000 requests per hour; check your institution's Canvas settings
For production integrations with multiple users, use the OAuth2 flow instead

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's Canvas profile
